rulings.Case Title: WEST BUILDERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To File Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Wednesday , February 5, 2025 Line 1. 2 - DEFENDANT STEADFAST INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To File Under Seal (Transaction Id # 75251635) Filed By Non-Party Zurich American Insurance Company. Off calendar. Request for dismissal on file. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday , February 5, 2025 Line 4. DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BOARD OF POLICE COMMISSIONERS,and WILLIAM SCOTT DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants City and County of San Francisco, Board of Police Commissioners and Police Chief William Scott's demurrer is continued to February 27, 2015, at 9:00 am in this department. Plaintiff San Francisco Police Officers' Association's objection to the Bar Association of San Francisco's amicus brief is overruled. Plaintiff's objection to the San Francisco Public Defender's amicus brief is overruled, provided that the San Francisco Public Defender's office provide a courtesy copy of its papers to the department no later than February 7, 2025, at noon. Each party to the case may file a supplemental brief not to exceed 15 pages of text (e.g., not including tables) responding to the amicus briefs. Such briefs must be filed no later than 4:00 pm on February 19, 2025, with a courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing. All involved are reminded that this is a demurrer in which the record is limited to the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed and the legal question is limited to whether challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a cause for relief. (See Code Civ. Proc., 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday , February 5, 2025 Line 6. PLAINTIFF ELISA ROSSI's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Plaintiff Elisa Rossi's Motion to Admit Counsel Pro Hac Vice, which is unopposed, is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Counsel Robert L. Lindholm declares only that in the past two years, he has not applied to appear pro hac vice may appear as counsel "in the Superior Court in the State of California." Rule 9.40, however requires that he identifies "[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 9.30(d)(5).) In other words, the information required is not limited to matters in the Superior Court. Plaintiff may file a new application with a corrected declaration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)
